rationale: The citing paper studies who shares political fake news on Twitter using users’ post-history language, prediction models, and survey interventions, while the cited paper analyzes FTC shutdown effects of fake-news advertising on consumer product-site traffic in 2010–2011. This is a substantial setting/outcome mismatch, and the only match evidence is a single DOI reference with no contextual citation text indicating method, theory, or direct comparison use. Based on the provided evidence, this looks most like a background mention rather than substantive dependence.
